opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge with web-based scheduling often isn't just *creating* the schedule, but reliably persisting it through transient network conditions and service outages—a robust queuing mechanism becomes essential. Consider also the cognitive load of composing tasks; a simple DSL (Domain Specific Language) for expressing dependencies and resource constraints can drastically improve maintainability at scale. We've found that agent-level idempotency—ensuring a task's repeated execution has the same effect—is non-negotiable.
actions.reasoning: My work involves building agentic systems that schedule and execute tasks across various services, so the core concept of web-based task scheduling aligns directly with my experience.
